appointment-type.title: What would you like to discuss?

options.hint: Explains the options for taking money from your defined contribution pension.
Divorce and dissolution can be complicated and stressful. Getting impartial guidance from us can make managing your pension options easier.
Speak to a pensions specialist if you are self-employed and need help understanding your pension options.
For people who have lost pension money due to a scam or fraud and need support.

radio-button.error: Select what type of appointment you would like.

sections.content: A free Pension Wise appointment explains the options for taking money from your defined contribution pension. It’s impartial and backed by government.
You’ll find out:
items: when you can access your pension pots
the different ways you can take money from your pension
how each option is usually taxed
how to spot and avoid scams.

sections.content: You can’t book a Pension Wise appointment if:
items: you’re under 50
you only have a defined benefit pension (final salary or career-average salary) or
you only have an overseas pension.

sections.content: If you’re self-employed, pension options can feel complicated and hard to compare. This appointment helps you understand how pensions work when you work for yourself, and what your choices are.
You’ll find out:
items: the types of pensions available to self-employed people
how contributions work and what you can afford to save
how tax relief is applied and what you could get back
what to consider when choosing a pension provider or scheme
how to check your state pension forecast
how to spot risks and avoid pension scams
where to get free, impartial guidance if you need more help

sections.content: You **can’t** book a Self-employed appointment if:
items: you aren’t self-employed

sections.content:
Our divorce and separation section is here to give you all the guidance you need when it comes to managing your finances during a relationship break-up.

It doesn’t matter what your situation is. You may be divorcing or separating; have children or not; own your home or rent; or live in England, Scotland, Wales or Northern Ireland. We have guides that will cover your specific situation.

Divorce and dissolution can be complicated and stressful. Getting impartial guidance from us can make managing your pension options easier.

During your appointment, you'll speak with one of our pension specialists who will:
items: explain the pension options available during a divorce/dissolution
help you understand what to consider and the questions to ask
guide you on your next steps, including where to find additional help and how to access regulated financial advice if needed.

sections.content: You **can’t** book a divorce and separation appointment if:
items: you have agreed a financial settlement in the divorce proceedings

sections.content:
There is scams support out there, but it can be overwhelming to navigate when you’ve been scammed. It can also come at a price that people cannot afford.

That’s why we have a specialist free Pension Loss service. Our pension specialists help consumers to make sense of what has happened and all the bewildering acronyms. We discuss the possible redress options and provide helpful resources at no charge. This can be enough to enable a scams victim to approach the right organisations to recover at least some of the money they have lost.
You’ll find out:
items: what has happened to your pension and what it means for you
what common scam-related pension terms and acronyms refer to
what options you may have to recover some of the money you’ve lost
what to expect next, including possible investigations or follow-up steps

sections.content: You **can’t** book a pension loss appointment if:
items: you have not been the victim of a suspected or confirmed pension scam

pension-wise-pre-appointment.title: What you need for your Pension Wise appointment

sections.content: So we can understand your plans for retirement, we’ll usually ask you about your pensions and other finances. You’ll usually benefit more from a Pension Wise appointment if, before your appointment, you:
**Ask each of your pension providers:**
items: how much your pension is currently worth
if your pension has any special features, like a guaranteed pot value or annuity rate.

sections.content: **Check your State Pension forecast:**
items: [online on GOV.UK](https://www.gov.uk/check-state-pension) or
call [0800 731 0175](tel:08007310175) between 8am and 6pm, Monday to Friday.

sections.content: **Have details of your other finances, like:**
items: savings and investments
debts and repayments.

sections.content: **Think about your plans for retirement, such as:**
items: when you’d like to stop working
what type of pension income you’d like.

sections.content: On the next screen, you will be prompted to book your appointment date and time, but be mindful of having time to collate the information above to get the most out of your appointment.

self-employed-pre-appointment.title: What you need for your self-employed pensions appointment

sections.content: You do not need to have everything, but bringing any of the following will help us give you clearer guidance:
items: any pension statements you already have
details of pension schemes you’ve paid into before
information about any savings or investments linked to your retirement
your State Pension forecast, if you have one

Ask each of your pension providers:

pension-loss-pre-appointment.title: What you need for your pension loss appointment

sections.content: You do not need to have everything listed, but bringing any of the following can help us understand your situation more quickly:
items: Any letters, emails or messages you received about the pension investment or transfer
Copies of forms you signed, including transfer or investment paperwork
Statements from your pension provider showing recent activity or transactions
Screenshots of adverts, websites or social media posts linked to the scam
The name and contact details of anyone you dealt with
Any references, policy numbers or pension scheme details you have
Notes about what happened and when, including dates of calls or messages

eligibility-defined-contribution.title: Do you have a defined contribution pension?

sections.content: This is the type of pension where you or your employer pay into a pot, and its value can go up or down depending on investments. You can use our tool to [find out your pension type (opens in a new tab)](https://tool.moneyhelper.org.uk/en/pension-type/question-1).

eligibility-uk-pensions.title: Do you have any pensions based in the UK?

sections.content: You can book an appointment if you have at least one UK-based pension. For example, a pension you built up while working or living in the UK.

radio-button.label: Do you have any pensions based in the UK?
radio-button.error: Please select whether you only have an overseas pension

options.text: You have inherited someone else’s pension
You are retiring early due to poor health
Your pension scheme allows early access (protected pension age)

radio-button.error: Please select whether any of the options apply to you

Partner in a business partnership (declare income through Self Assessment)

sections.content: This appointment is only for people who have been the victim of pension fraud.

pension-wise-not-eligible.title: You’re not eligible for a Pension Wise appointment

sections.content: You can’t book a Pension Wise appointment if:
items: you’re under 50
you only have a defined benefit pension (final salary or career-average salary) or
you only have an overseas pension.

sections.content: We can still help if you’re not eligible for Pension Wise. You can contact our pension specialists for free guidance:
items: [use our webchat](#)
[use our online form](#)

sections.content: We’re open between 9am and 5pm, Monday to Friday. Closed on bank holidays.
You can also use our [pension guides and tools.](#)

access-support.title: Would you like any adjustments for your appointment?

sections.content: A standard PensionWise appointment will be via telephone and last for approximately 1 hour. We can make adjustments to meet your needs. For example, you can ask for:
items: A Welsh-speaking pension specialist
A British Sign Language interpreter
A foreign language interpreter
Someone to attend the appointment with you
Adjustments for accessibility needs

radio-button.error: Select whether you would like adjustments for your appointment.

access-options.title: What would help make your appointment easier?

options.text: Someone to attend the appointment with you
options.hint: We can use a conference call so they can join and provide support, even if they’re in a different location to you

radio-button.error: Select whether you would like adjustments for your appointment.

text-area.label: Anything else that would help? (Optional)
text-area.hint: Tell us if there’s anything else that would make your appointment easier (for example, if you’re neurodivergent or prefer information explained slowly).

access-language.title: What language do you need an interpreter for?

sections.content: We can arrange an interpreter for most spoken languages. Choose your preferred language from the list below. If your language isn’t shown, select ‘Other language’ and tell us which one you need.

language-type.error: Please select what language you need an interpreter for.

language-other.label: Other language (please specify)
language-other.error: Please specify which language you need an interpreter for.

help.title: Need Help?
help.content:
Speak to someone who can help book your free appointment on [0800 138 3944](tel:08001383944) or use our [webchat](https://webchat.moneyhelper.org.uk/newchat/chat.aspx?domain=www.moneyhelper.org.uk).

Call between 8am to 6:30pm, Monday to Friday.
